answer:
In Charles Dunoyer's *Nouveau traité d'économie sociale* (Vol. I), the Introduction (Chapter 0) announces and outlines the author's intent to define *liberté* (liberty), but the explicit definition appears immediately afterward in Chapter 1 ("What the author means by the word liberty").[^1]

Dunoyer defines *liberty* as follows:

> What I call *liberty*, in this book, is this *power* that man acquires to use his forces more easily *as he frees himself* from the obstacles that originally hindered their exercise. I say that he is all the more *free* as he is more *delivered* from the causes that prevented him from making use of them, as he has further removed these causes from himself, as he has more enlarged and cleared the sphere of his action.[^2]

He emphasizes that this usage aligns with the word's "most usual and familiar sense," as found in thinkers like Locke, Condillac, and Destutt de Tracy, equating it to *power* over one's faculties once internal (e.g., ignorance, vice) and external obstacles (e.g., injustice by others) are overcome.[^3] He reiterates: "the word liberty corresponds, in my thought, to the idea of *power*, and [...] the phenomenon I wish to designate by it is that ever-increasing power to act which manifests itself in us as we succeed in ridding our faculties of some of the obstacles that prevented us from making use of them."[^4]

Dunoyer ties this power to human progress in industry (skill) and morality throughout the work.[^5]

[^1]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*, [Chapter 0](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/00-introduction/translation/).

[^2]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*, [paragraph](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/01-ce-que-lauteur-entend-par-le-mot-liberte/translation/#p-fc8999).

[^3]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*, [paragraph](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/01-ce-que-lauteur-entend-par-le-mot-liberte/translation/#p-ed4810).

[^4]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*, [paragraph](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/01-ce-que-lauteur-entend-par-le-mot-liberte/translation/#p-51f10e).

[^5]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*, [Chapter 1](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/01-ce-que-lauteur-entend-par-le-mot-liberte/translation/).
